revision.text: #redirect[[en:Shudenryō]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''主殿寮'''(しゅでんりょう・とのもりのつかさ)は[[律令制]]において[[宮内省]]に属した機関。'''とのもりょう'''・'''とのもりづかさ'''とも読む。
== 職掌 == 主殿寮は[[内裏]]・消耗品の管理・供給を主な職掌とした。特に施設管理業務には人手がかかったため駆使丁や今良など大量の人員を擁した。[[掃部寮]]と職掌が重なる部分もある。808年には[[官奴司]]を併合して官奴婢・[[官戸]]の管理も行った。同様の官司として春宮坊の[[主殿署]]､後宮の[[殿司]]､斎宮寮の[[殿部司]]などがある。 後に長官は[[小槻家]]が相続・世襲した。 == 職員 == *頭（従五位下） *助（従六位上) *充（従七位上） *大属（従八位下） 少属（大初位上）
*殿部 調度品・消耗品の管理・維持を行う伴部・古くは特定の氏から採られた
*使部 *直丁 *駆使丁
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]]
{{Japanese-history-stub|とのもりのつかさ}} [[category:律令制の官制|とのもりのつかさ]]

'''主殿寮'''（しゅでんりょう・とのもりのつかさ）は[[律令制#日本の律令制|律令制]]において[[宮内省]]に属した機関。'''とのもりょう'''・'''とのもりづかさ'''とも読む。
== 職掌 == 主殿寮は[[大内裏|内裏]]における消耗品の管理・供給を主な職掌とした。特に施設管理業務には人手がかかったため駆使丁や今良など大量の人員を擁した。[[掃部寮]]と職掌が重なる部分もある。808年には[[官奴司]]を併合して官奴婢・[[官戸]]の管理も行った。同様の官司として春宮坊の[[主殿署]]､後宮の[[殿司]]､斎宮寮の[[殿部司]]などがある。 後に長官は[[小槻氏]]が相続・世襲した。 == 職員 == *頭（従五位下相当） *助（従六位上相当) *充（従七位上相当） *大属（従八位下相当） 少属（大初位上相当）
*殿部 調度品・消耗品の管理・維持を行う伴部・古くは特定の氏から採用された
*使部 *直丁 *駆使丁
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]]
[[category:廃止された日本の国家機関|とのもりのつかさ]] [[category:律令制の官制|とのもりのつかさ]] {{Japanese-history-stub|とのもりのつかさ}}

revision.comment: 律令制や幕府の機関はきりがないから“廃止された日本の国家機関” のカテゴリーから外した方がいい。

'''主殿寮'''（しゅでんりょう・とのもりのつかさ）は[[律令制#日本の律令制|律令制]]において[[宮内省]]に属した機関。'''とのもりょう'''・'''とのもりづかさ'''とも読む。
== 職掌 == 主殿寮は[[大内裏|内裏]]における消耗品の管理・供給を主な職掌とした。特に施設管理業務には人手がかかったため駆使丁や今良など大量の人員を擁した。[[掃部寮]]と職掌が重なる部分もある。808年には[[官奴司]]を併合して官奴婢・[[官戸]]の管理も行った。同様の官司として春宮坊の[[主殿署]]､後宮の[[殿司]]､斎宮寮の[[殿部司]]などがある。 後に長官は[[小槻氏]]が相続・世襲した。 == 職員 == *頭（従五位下相当） *助（従六位上相当) *充（従七位上相当） *大属（従八位下相当） 少属（大初位上相当）
*殿部 調度品・消耗品の管理・維持を行う伴部・古くは特定の氏から採用された
*使部 *直丁 *駆使丁
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]]
[[category:律令制の官制|とのもりのつかさ]] {{Japanese-history-stub|とのもりのつかさ}}

'''主殿寮'''（しゅでんりょう・とのもりのつかさ）は[[律令制#日本の律令制|律令制]]において[[宮内省]]に属した機関。'''とのもりょう'''・'''とのもりづかさ'''とも読む。
== 職掌 == 主殿寮は[[大内裏|内裏]]における消耗品の管理・供給を主な職掌とした。特に施設管理業務には人手がかかったため駆使丁や今良など大量の人員を擁した。[[掃部寮]]と職掌が重なる部分もある。808年には[[官奴司]]を併合して官奴婢・[[官戸]]の管理も行った。同様の官司として春宮坊の[[主殿署]]、後宮の[[殿司]]、斎宮寮の[[殿部司]]などがある。
後に長官は[[小槻氏]]が相続・世襲した。
== 職員 == *頭（従五位下相当） *助（従六位上相当) *允（従七位上相当） *大属（従八位下相当） 少属（大初位上相当）
*殿部 調度品・消耗品の管理・維持を行う伴部・古くは特定の氏から採用された
*使部 *直丁 *駆使丁
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とのもりのつかさ}} [[category:律令制の官制]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
'''主殿寮'''（とのもりょう・しゅでんりょう・とのもづかさ・とのもりづかさ）は、[[律令制#日本の律令制|律令制]]において[[宮内省]]に属した機関。
== 職掌 == 主殿寮は[[大内裏|内裏]]における[[消耗品]]の管理・供給を主な職掌とした。特に施設管理業務には人手がかかったため駆使丁や今良など大量の人員を擁した。[[掃部寮]]と職掌が重なる部分もある。[[808年]]（大同3年）には[[官奴司]]を併合して官奴婢・[[官戸]]の管理も行った。同様の官司として[[春宮坊]]の[[主殿署]]、[[後宮]]の[[殿司]]、[[斎宮寮]]の[[殿部司]]などがある。
後に長官は[[小槻氏]]が相続・世襲した。
== 職員 == * 頭（[[従五位|従五位下]]相当） * 助（[[従六位|従六位上]]相当) * 允（[[従七位|従七位上]]相当） * 大属（[[従八位|従八位下]]相当） 少属（[[大初位|大初位上]]相当）
* 殿部 調度品・[[消耗品]]の管理・維持を行う伴部・古くは特定の氏から採用された
* 使部 * 直丁 * 駆使丁
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とのもりのつかさ}} [[category:律令制の官制]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''主殿寮'''（とのもりょう・しゅでんりょう・とのもづかさ・とのもりづかさ）は、[[律令制#日本の律令制|律令制]]において[[宮内省 (律令制)|宮内省]]に属した機関。
== 職掌 == 主殿寮は[[大内裏|内裏]]における[[消耗品]]の管理・供給を主な職掌とした。特に施設管理業務には人手がかかったため駆使丁や今良など大量の人員を擁した。[[掃部寮]]と職掌が重なる部分もある。[[808年]]（大同3年）には[[官奴司]]を併合して官奴婢・[[官戸]]の管理も行った。同様の官司として[[春宮坊]]の[[主殿署]]、[[後宮]]の[[殿司]]、[[斎宮寮]]の[[殿部司]]などがある。
「とのも」の名は、御殿（との）の管理を役職とする主殿守（とのもり）からと推測されている<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[平凡社]]、2007年</ref>。
後に長官は[[小槻氏]]が相続・世襲した。
== 職員 == * 頭（[[従五位|従五位下]]相当） * 助（[[従六位|従六位上]]相当) * 允（[[従七位|従七位上]]相当） * 大属（[[従八位|従八位下]]相当） 少属（[[大初位|大初位上]]相当）
* 殿部 調度品・[[消耗品]]の管理・維持を行う伴部・古くは特定の氏から採用された
* 使部 * 直丁 * 駆使丁
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とのもりのつかさ}} [[category:律令制の官制]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a..."
revision.text:
'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * **Tō** (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * **Suke** (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * **Jō** (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * **Daisakan** (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * **Shōsakan** (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
### Subordinate Roles * **Denbu** (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * **Shibu** (使部) * **Chokuchō** (直丁) * **Kushichō** (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shudenryō}} [[Category:Ritsuryō system government offices]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * **Tō** (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * **Suke** (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * **Jō** (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * **Daisakan** (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * **Shōsakan** (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
### Subordinate Roles * **Denbu** (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * **Shibu** (使部) * **Chokuchō** (直丁) * **Kushichō** (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shudenryō}} [[Category:Ritsuryō system government offices]] [[Category:Original pages]][[ja:主殿寮]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
### Subordinate Roles * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.

revision.text:
'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
### Subordinate Roles * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

revision.text:
'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
### Subordinate Roles * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').

== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
### Subordinate Roles * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
### Subordinate Roles * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
### Subordinate Roles * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
### Subordinate Roles * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
### Subordinate Roles * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Shudenryō]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:主殿寮]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:New]]

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

revision.text:
'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes ==

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

17 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:主殿寮 up to rev 100290490
revision.text:
'''主殿寮'''（とのもりょう・しゅでんりょう・とのもづかさ・とのもりづかさ）は、[[律令制#日本の律令制|律令制]]において[[宮内省 (律令制)|宮内省]]に属した機関。
== 職掌 == 主殿寮は[[大内裏|内裏]]における[[消耗品]]の管理・供給を主な職掌とした。特に施設管理業務には人手がかかったため駆使丁や今良など大量の人員を擁した。[[掃部寮]]と職掌が重なる部分もある。[[808年]]（大同3年）には[[官奴司]]を併合して官奴婢・[[官戸]]の管理も行った。同様の官司として[[春宮坊]]の[[主殿署]]、[[後宮]]の[[殿司]]、[[斎宮寮]]の[[殿部司]]などがある。
「とのも」の名は、御殿（との）の管理を役職とする主殿守（とのもり）からと推測されている<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[平凡社]]、2007年</ref>。
後に長官は[[小槻氏]]が相続・世襲した。
== 職員 == * 頭（[[従五位|従五位下]]相当） * 助（[[従六位|従六位上]]相当) * 允（[[従七位|従七位上]]相当） * 大属（[[従八位|従八位下]]相当） 少属（[[大初位|大初位上]]相当）
* 殿部 調度品・[[消耗品]]の管理・維持を行う伴部・古くは特定の氏から採用された
* 使部 * 直丁 * 駆使丁
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とのもりのつかさ}} [[category:律令制の官制]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[主殿寮]] to [[Shudenryō]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

'''主殿寮'''（とのもりょう・しゅでんりょう・とのもづかさ・とのもりづかさ）は、[[律令制#日本の律令制|律令制]]において[[宮内省 (律令制)|宮内省]]に属した機関。
== 職掌 == 主殿寮は[[大内裏|内裏]]における[[消耗品]]の管理・供給を主な職掌とした。特に施設管理業務には人手がかかったため駆使丁や今良など大量の人員を擁した。[[掃部寮]]と職掌が重なる部分もある。[[808年]]（大同3年）には[[官奴司]]を併合して官奴婢・[[官戸]]の管理も行った。同様の官司として[[春宮坊]]の[[主殿署]]、[[後宮]]の[[殿司]]、[[斎宮寮]]の[[殿部司]]などがある。
「とのも」の名は、御殿（との）の管理を役職とする主殿守（とのもり）からと推測されている<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[平凡社]]、2007年</ref>。
後に長官は[[小槻氏]]が相続・世襲した。
== 職員 == * 頭（[[従五位|従五位下]]相当） * 助（[[従六位|従六位上]]相当) * 允（[[従七位|従七位上]]相当） * 大属（[[従八位|従八位下]]相当） 少属（[[大初位|大初位上]]相当）
* 殿部 調度品・[[消耗品]]の管理・維持を行う伴部・古くは特定の氏から採用された
* 使部 * 直丁 * 駆使丁
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とのもりのつかさ}} [[category:律令制の官制]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[:en:808|808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Greater Initial Rank|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.

In [[:en:808|808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[:en:Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Senior Initial Rank|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]

=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes ==

== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[:en:Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In [[:en:808|808]] (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the [[:en:Saigū|Saikū Bureau]].
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Senior Initial Rank|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:主殿寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:主殿寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:主殿寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11368767]] Wikidata name: Tonomo-ryō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shudenryō''' (主殿寮, also read as ''Tonoryō'', ''Shudenryō'', ''Tonotsukasa'', or ''Tonomoritsukasa'') was an institution under the [[:en:Kunaishō|Ministry of the Imperial Household]] during the [[Ritsuryō system]] in Japan.
== Functions == The Shudenryō was primarily responsible for managing and supplying consumable goods within the [[:en:Daijōkan|Inner Palace]] (''Dairi''). Due to the labor-intensive nature of maintaining palace facilities, the bureau employed a large workforce, including ''Kushichō'' (servant workers) and ''Imayoshi''. Its functions overlapped in part with the [[Sōboryō]] (掃部寮). In {{ill|808|en|808|WD=Q399792|lt=808}} (Daidō 3), the Shudenryō absorbed the [[Kannushi|Kannushi Bureau]] and took over the management of government slaves (''kannuhi'') and state households (''kanto'').
Similar bureaus included the [[Shutensha]] (主殿署) in the [[Crown Prince's Palace]] (''Tōgū''), the [[Denfu]] (殿司) in the [[Kōgōgū|Empress's Palace]], and the [[Tenbushi]] (殿部司) in the {{ill|Saigū|en|Saigū|WD=Q123417769|lt=Saikū Bureau}}.
The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}, 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.
== Staff == * '''Tō''' (頭) – Equivalent to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * '''Suke''' (助) – Equivalent to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上) * '''Jō''' (允) – Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上) * '''Daisakan''' (大属) – Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下) * '''Shōsakan''' (少属) – Equivalent to [[Senior Initial Rank|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上)
=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Japanese Ritsuryō System]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|主殿寮|version=100290490|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The name "Tonomo" likely derives from ''tonomori'' (殿守), meaning caretakers of palace buildings (''tonos'').<ref>『改訂新版 世界大百科事典』{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}, 2007.</ref> Later, the head of the bureau (''Tō'') was inherited by the [[Otsuki clan]] (小槻氏), establishing a hereditary succession.

=== Subordinate Roles === * '''Denbu''' (殿部) – Managed and maintained furnishings and consumables; traditionally recruited from specific families. * '''Shibu''' (使部) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁) * '''Kushichō''' (駆使丁) – Labor workers.
